RegisterDescription.ToolTip: IDS Imaging Development Systems GmbH uEye remote device (interface)

Category.Name: DeviceControl
Category.ToolTip: Category for Device information and control
Category.Description: Category for Device information and control.

Category.Name: ImageFormatControl
Category.ToolTip: Category for Image Format Control features
Category.Description: Category for Image Format Control features.

Category.Name: SubRegionControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains all parameters for setting the sub regions.
Category.Description: Category that contains all parameters for setting the sub regions.

Category.Name: AcquisitionControl
Category.ToolTip: Category for the acquisition and trigger control features
Category.Description: Category for the acquisition and trigger control features.

Category.Name: AnalogControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains the Analog control features
Category.Description: Category that contains the Analog control features.

Category.Name: BrightnessAutoControl
Category.ToolTip: Category for extended control over the automatic brightness features ExposureAuto and GainAuto.
Category.Description: Category for extended control over the automatic brightness features ExposureAuto and GainAuto. BrightnessAutoExposureTimeLimitMode, BrightnessAutoExposureTimeMin and BrightnessAutoExposureTimeMax allow to precisely define the allowed range of the exposure time for automatic adjustments, when ExposureAuto is active. BrightnessAutoGainLimitMode, BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax allow to precisely define the allowed range for gain for automatic adjustments, when GainAuto is active. With the features BrightnessAutoPercentile, BrightnessAutoTarget and BrightnessAutoTargetTolerance the algorithms of the automatic brightness features are adapted. This way, the camera image is kept brighter or darker - depending on your application.

Category.Name: ImageCorrectionControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains all features related to image correction
Category.Description: Category that contains all features related to image correction.

Category.Name: FlashControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains all features related to flash
Category.Description: Category that contains all features related to flash.

Category.Name: DigitalIOControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains the digital input and output control features
Category.Description: Category that contains the digital input and output control features.

Category.Name: PWMControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains all settings regarding the available PWM modules
Category.Description: Category that contains all settings regarding the available PWM modules.

Category.Name: TransportLayerControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains the Transport layer control features
Category.Description: Category that contains the Transport layer control features.

Category.Name: UserSetControl
Category.ToolTip: Global control of the device settings. It allows loading or saving factory or user-defined settings
Category.Description: Global control of the device settings. It allows loading or saving factory or user-defined settings.

Category.Name: UEyeParametersetControl
Category.Description: Controls for saving and loading uEye paremeterset files (ini-files).

Category.Name: FileAccessControl
Category.ToolTip: Category that contains all parameters related to accessing files in the camera.
Category.Description: Category that contains all parameters related to accessing files in the camera.

StringReg.Name: DeviceFamilyName
StringReg.ToolTip: Identifier of the product family of the camera.
StringReg.Description: Identifier of the product family of the camera.

StringReg.Name: DeviceSerialNumber
StringReg.ToolTip: Returns the serial number of the camera.
StringReg.Description: Returns the serial number of the camera. This string is a unique identifier of the camera.

Integer.Name: DeviceUserIDuEye
Integer.ToolTip: User-programmable identifier
Integer.Description: User-programmable device identification. This represents the uEye camera ID and can be used for device selection via the DeviceUserID node.

Enumeration.Name: DeviceTemperatureSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the location within the camera, where the temperature is measured.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the location within the camera, where the temperature is measured.

EnumEntry.Name: Mainboard
EnumEntry.Description: Temperature of the camera's mainboard.

EnumEntry.Name: Sensor
EnumEntry.Description: Temperature of the camera's sensor.

Float.Name: DeviceTemperature
Float.ToolTip: Camera temperature in degrees Celsius (C).
Float.Description: Camera temperature in degrees Celsius (C). It is measured at the location selected by DeviceTemperatureSelector.

Enumeration.Name: DeviceClockSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns the source for DeviceClockFrequency.
Enumeration.Description: Returns the source for DeviceClockFrequency.

EnumEntry.Name: Sensor
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Clock frequency of the image sensor of the camera.
EnumEntry.Description: Clock frequency of the image sensor of the camera.

Float.Name: DeviceClockFrequency
Float.ToolTip: Controls the frequency of the clock (in Hertz) selected in DeviceClockSelector.
Float.Description: Controls the frequency of the clock (in Hertz) selected in DeviceClockSelector. Changing DeviceClockFrequency also changes ExposureTime and AcquisitionFrameRate. For camera models with a discrete list of valid values, where the increment between two valid values is non-constant, the implementation will choose a suitable setting based on the desired value, the current value, and the list of valid values.

Command.Name: DeviceReset
Command.ToolTip: Resets the camera to its power-up state.
Command.Description: Resets the camera to its power-up state. After reset, the camera must be rediscovered.

Integer.Name: DeviceLinkSpeed
Integer.ToolTip: Note that this represents the total speed of all the connections of the Link.
Integer.Description: Indicates the speed of transmission negotiated on the specified Link.

Integer.Name: SensorWidth
Integer.ToolTip: Effective width of the sensor in pixels.
Integer.Description: Effective width of the sensor in pixels.

Integer.Name: SensorHeight
Integer.ToolTip: Effective height of the sensor in pixels.
Integer.Description: Effective height of the sensor in pixels.

Float.Name: SensorPixelWidth
Float.ToolTip: Returns the physical pixel size (in um) in x direction.
Float.Description: Returns the physical pixel size (in um) in x direction.

Float.Name: SensorPixelHeight
Float.ToolTip: Returns the physical pixel size (in um) in y direction.
Float.Description: Returns the physical pixel size (in um) in y direction.

Integer.Name: WidthMax
Integer.ToolTip: Maximum width of the image (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Maximum width of the image (in pixels).

Integer.Name: HeightMax
Integer.ToolTip: Maximum height of the image (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Maximum height of the image (in pixels).

Integer.Name: Width
Integer.ToolTip: Width of the Image provided by the device (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Width of the Image provided by the device (in pixels).

Integer.Name: Height
Integer.ToolTip: Height of the image provided by the device (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Height of the image provided by the device (in pixels).

Register.Name: UEyeROI
Register.ToolTip: Writes the ROI within an instruction
Register.Description: Writes the ROI within an instruction. The data is in the order 'OffsetX', 'OffsetY', 'Width', 'Height' with 4 bytes each.

Integer.Name: OffsetX
Integer.ToolTip: Horizontal offset from the origin to the region of interest (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Horizontal offset from the origin to the region of interest (in pixels).

Integer.Name: OffsetY
Integer.ToolTip: Vertical offset from the origin to the region of interest (in pixels).
Integer.Description: Vertical offset from the origin to the region of interest (in pixels).

Enumeration.Name: BinningSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which binning engine is controlled by the BinningHorizontal and BinningVertical features.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which binning engine is controlled by the BinningHorizontal and BinningVertical features.

EnumEntry.Name: uEye
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selected features will control the uEye binning.
EnumEntry.Description: Selected features will control the uEye binning. The processor depends on the uEye camera model.

Enumeration.Name: BinningHorizontalMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Pixel combination mode for horizontal binning.
Enumeration.Description: Pixel combination mode when BinningHorizontal is active.

EnumEntry.Name: uEye
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The binning mode depends on the uEye camera model.
EnumEntry.Description: The binning mode depends on the uEye camera model.

Integer.Name: BinningHorizontal
Integer.ToolTip: Number of horizontal photo-sensitive cells to combine together.
Integer.Description: Number of horizontal photo-sensitive cells to combine together. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image. Note for camera models of type UI-124x/UI-324x/UI-524x, UI-125x/UI-325x/UI-525x, and UI-155x/UI-555x: The horizontal and vertical binning factors can only be set combined. I.e. BinningVertical is automatically adjusted to the same value as BinningHorizontal.

Enumeration.Name: UEyeBinningFactorHorizontal
Enumeration.ToolTip: Number of horizontal photo-sensitive cells to combine together.
Enumeration.Description: Number of horizontal photo-sensitive cells to combine together. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image. Note for camera models of type UI-124x/UI-324x/UI-524x, UI-125x/UI-325x/UI-525x, and UI-155x/UI-555x: The horizontal and vertical binning factors can only be set combined. I.e. BinningVertical is automatically adjusted to the same value as BinningHorizontal.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor1
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 1.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor2
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 2.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor3
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 3.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor4
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 4.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor5
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 5.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor6
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 6.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor8
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 8.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor16
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 16.

Enumeration.Name: BinningVerticalMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Pixel combination mode for vertical binning.
Enumeration.Description: Pixel combination mode when BinningVertical is active.

EnumEntry.Name: uEye
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The binning mode depends on the uEye camera model.
EnumEntry.Description: The binning mode depends on the uEye camera model.

Integer.Name: BinningVertical
Integer.ToolTip: Number of vertical photo-sensitive cells to combine together.
Integer.Description: Number of vertical photo-sensitive cells to combine together. This reduces the vertical resolution (height) of the image. Note for camera models of type UI-124x/UI-324x/UI-524x, UI-125x/UI-325x/UI-525x, and UI-155x/UI-555x: The horizontal and vertical binning factors can only be set combined. I.e. BinningHorizontal is automatically adjusted to the same value as BinningVertical.

Enumeration.Name: UEyeBinningFactorVertical
Enumeration.ToolTip: Number of vertical photo-sensitive cells to combine together.
Enumeration.Description: Number of vertical photo-sensitive cells to combine together. This reduces the vertical resolution (height) of the image. Note for camera models of type UI-124x/UI-324x/UI-524x, UI-125x/UI-325x/UI-525x, and UI-155x/UI-555x: The horizontal and vertical binning factors can only be set combined. I.e. BinningHorizontal is automatically adjusted to the same value as BinningVertical.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor1
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 1.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor2
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 2.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor3
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 3.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor4
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 4.

EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 5.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor6
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 6.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor8
EnumEntry.Description: Binning factor 8.

EnumEntry.Name: Factor16

Enumeration.Name: DecimationSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which decimation engine is controlled by the DecimationHorizontal and DecimationVertical features.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which decimation engine is controlled by the DecimationHorizontal and DecimationVertical features.

EnumEntry.Name: uEye

EnumEntry.Description: Selected features will control the uEye subsampling. The processor depends on the uEye camera model.

Enumeration.Name: DecimationHorizontalMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Mode used to reduce the horizontal resolution when DecimationHorizontal is used.
Enumeration.Description: Mode used to reduce the horizontal resolution when DecimationHorizontal is used.

EnumEntry.ToolTip: The value of every Nth pixel is kept, others are discarded.
EnumEntry.Description: The value of every Nth pixel is kept, others are discarded.

Integer.ToolTip: Horizontal sub-sampling of the image.
Integer.Description: Horizontal sub-sampling of the image. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image by the specified horizontal decimation factor.

Enumeration.Description: Horizontal sub-sampling of the image. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image by the specified horizontal decimation factor.

EnumEntry.Name: Discard
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The value of every Nth pixel is kept, others are discarded.
EnumEntry.Description: The value of every Nth pixel is kept, others are discarded.

Integer.Description: Vertical sub-sampling of the image. This reduces the vertical resolution (height) of the image by the specified vertical decimation factor.

Enumeration.ToolTip: Vertical sub-sampling of the image.

Enumeration.Name: ScalingSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which scaler engine is controlled by the ScalingHorizontal and ScalingVertical features.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which scaler engine is controlled by the scaling related features.

EnumEntry.Name: Sensor
EnumEntry.Description: Selected features control Sensor scaler.

Float.Name: ScalingHorizontal
Float.ToolTip: Horizontal scaling factor
Float.Description: Horizontal scaling factor. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image. The vertical and horizontal scaling factors can only be set combined. I.e. ScalingVertical is automatically adjusted to the same value as ScalingHorizontal.

Float.Name: ScalingVertical
Float.ToolTip: Vertical scaling factor
Float.Description: Horizontal scaling factor. This reduces the horizontal resolution (width) of the image. The horizontal and vertical scaling factors can only be set combined. I.e. ScalingHorizontal is automatically adjusted to the same value as ScalingVertical.

Boolean.Name: ReverseX
Boolean.ToolTip: Flip horizontally the image sent by the device.
Boolean.Description: Flip horizontally the image sent by the device.

Boolean.Name: ReverseY
Boolean.ToolTip: Flip vertically the image sent by the device.
Boolean.Description: Flip vertically the image sent by the device.

Enumeration.Name: PixelFormat
Enumeration.ToolTip: Format of the pixels provided by the device.
Enumeration.Description: Format of the pixels provided by the device. It represents all the information provided by PixelSize and PixelColorFilter combined in a single feature.

EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Blue-Green 10-bit.

EnumEntry.Name: BayerBG12
EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Blue-Green 12-bit.

EnumEntry.Name: BGRa8

EnumEntry.Name: BGR8
EnumEntry.Description: BGR (8 8 8), occupies 24 bits

Enumeration.Name: PixelSize
Enumeration.ToolTip: Total size in bits of a pixel of the image.
Enumeration.Description: Total size in bits of a pixel of the image.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp1
EnumEntry.Description: 1 bit per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp2
EnumEntry.Description: 2 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp4
EnumEntry.Description: 4 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp8
EnumEntry.Description: 8 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp10
EnumEntry.Description: 10 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp12
EnumEntry.Description: 12 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp16
EnumEntry.Description: 16 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp24
EnumEntry.Description: 24 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp30
EnumEntry.Description: 30 bits per pixel.

EnumEntry.Name: Bpp32
EnumEntry.Description: 32 bits per pixel.

Enumeration.Name: PixelColorFilter
Enumeration.ToolTip: Type of color filter that is applied to the image.
Enumeration.Description: Type of color filter that is applied to the image.

EnumEntry.Name: None
EnumEntry.Description: No color filter.

EnumEntry.Name: BayerRG
EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Red Green filter.

EnumEntry.Name: BayerGB
EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Green Blue filter.

EnumEntry.Name: BayerGR
EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Green Red filter.

EnumEntry.Name: BayerBG
EnumEntry.Description: Bayer Blue Green filter.

Enumeration.Name: TestPattern
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the type of test pattern that is generated by the device as image source.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the type of test pattern that is generated by the device as image source.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Image is coming from the sensor.
EnumEntry.Description: Image is coming from the sensor.

EnumEntry.Name: Black
EnumEntry.Description: Black image

EnumEntry.Name: White
EnumEntry.Description: White image

EnumEntry.Name: GreyHorizontalRamp
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Horizontal ramp with grey values from black to white.
EnumEntry.Description: Horizontal ramp with grey values from black to white.

EnumEntry.Name: GreyVerticalRamp
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Vertical ramp with grey values from black to white.
EnumEntry.Description: Vertical ramp with grey values from black to white.

EnumEntry.Name: GreyWedgeSensor
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Grey wedges generated by the image sensor.
EnumEntry.Description: Grey wedges generated by the image sensor.

EnumEntry.Name: GreyWedgeMovingSensor
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Moving grey wedges generated by the image sensor.
EnumEntry.Description: Moving grey wedges generated by the image sensor.

EnumEntry.Name: RampingPattern
EnumEntry.Description: Diagonal color pattern

EnumEntry.Name: ChessPattern
EnumEntry.Description: Chessboard pattern.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlBlack
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern Black.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlWhite
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern White.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlGreyHorizontalRamp
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern GreyHorizontalRamp.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlGreyVerticalRamp
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern GreyVerticalRamp.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlGreyWedgeSensor
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern GreyWedgeSensor.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlGreyWedgeMovingSensor
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern GreyWedgeMovingSensor.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlRampingPattern
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern RampingPattern.

Boolean.Name: TestPatternAvlChessPattern
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the image sensor provides the test pattern ChessPattern.

Command.Description: Loads the selected preset. Invalidates all size related nodes, so the acquisition must be stopped if it is running.

Enumeration.Name: AcquisitionMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets the acquisition mode of the camera.
Enumeration.Description: Sets the acquisition mode of the camera. It defines the number of images to capture during an acquisition. The access changes to read-only during an acquisition.

EnumEntry.Name: SingleFrame
EnumEntry.Description: One image is captured.

EnumEntry.Name: Continuous
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Images are captured until stopped with the AcquisitionStop command.
EnumEntry.Description: Images are captured until stopped with the AcquisitionStop command.

Command.Name: AcquisitionStart
Command.ToolTip: Starts the acquisition of the camera.
Command.Description: Starts the acquisition of the camera. The number of captured images is specified by AcquisitionMode.

Command.Name: AcquisitionStop
Command.ToolTip: Stops the acquisition of the camera at the end of the current image.
Command.Description: Stops the acquisition of the camera at the end of the current image. It is mainly used when AcquisitionMode is Continuous but can be used in any acquisition mode.

Float.Name: AcquisitionFrameRate
Float.ToolTip: Controls the acquisition rate (in Hertz) at which the images are captured.
Float.Description: Controls the acquisition rate (in Hertz) at which the images are captured. The value and range of AcquisitionFrameRate depend on the current value of DeviceClockFrequency.

Enumeration.Name: TriggerSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the type of trigger to configure.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the type of trigger to configure.

EnumEntry.Name: ExposureStart
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects a trigger controlling the start of the exposure of one image.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects a trigger controlling the start of the exposure of one image.

Enumeration.Name: TriggerMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls if the selected trigger is active.
Enumeration.Description: Controls if the selected trigger is active. The access changes to read-only during an acquisition.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Disables the selected trigger.

EnumEntry.Name: On
EnumEntry.Description: Enables the selected trigger.

Enumeration.Name: TriggerSource
Enumeration.ToolTip: Specifies the internal signal or physical input Line to use as the trigger source.
Enumeration.Description: Specifies the internal signal or physical input line to use as the trigger source. The selected trigger must have its TriggerMode set to On. The access changes to read-only during an acquisition.

EnumEntry.Name: Software
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies that the trigger source will be generated by software using the TriggerSoftware command.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies that the trigger source will be generated by software using the TriggerSoftware command.

EnumEntry.Name: Line0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use Line0 (trigger input) as external source for the trigger signal.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use Line0 (trigger input) as external source for the trigger signal. For interpreting the trigger signal, either the rising or the falling edge can be used.

EnumEntry.Name: Line2
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use the General Purpose I/O Line2 (GPIO1) as external source for the trigger signal.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use the General Purpose I/O Line2 (GPIO1) as external source for the trigger signal. For interpreting the trigger signal, either the rising or the falling edge can be used.

EnumEntry.Name: Line3
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use the General Purpose I/O Line3 (GPIO2) as external source for the trigger signal.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use the General Purpose I/O Line3 (GPIO2) as external source for the trigger signal. For interpreting the trigger signal, either the rising or the falling edge can be used.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use UserOutput0 as internal source for the trigger.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use UserOutput0 as internal source for the trigger. In this mode, signals on Line0 also trigger ExposureStart.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput1
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use UserOutput1 as internal source for the trigger.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use UserOutput1 as internal source for the trigger. In this mode, signals on Line0 also trigger ExposureStart.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput2
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use UserOutput2 as internal source for the trigger.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use UserOutput2 as internal source for the trigger. In this mode, signals on Line0 also trigger ExposureStart.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput3
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies to use UserOutput3 as internal source for the trigger.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies to use UserOutput3 as internal source for the trigger. In this mode, signals on Line0 also trigger ExposureStart.

Command.Name: TriggerSoftware
Command.Description: Generates an internal software trigger. TriggerSource must be set to Software.

Enumeration.Name: TriggerActivation
Enumeration.ToolTip: Specifies the activation mode of the trigger.
Enumeration.Description: Specifies the activation mode of the trigger. The access changes to read-only during an acquisition.

EnumEntry.Name: RisingEdge
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid on the rising edge of the source signal.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid on the rising edge of the source signal.

EnumEntry.Name: FallingEdge
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid on the falling edge of the source signal.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid on the falling edge of the source signal.

Boolean.Name: TriggerActivationAvlRisingEdge
Boolean.ToolTip: Availability of the trigger activation by rising edge
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the trigger activation by rising edge is available.

Boolean.Name: TriggerActivationAvlFallingEdge
Boolean.ToolTip: Availability of the trigger activation by falling edge
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the trigger activation by falling edge is available.

Float.Name: TriggerDelay
Float.ToolTip: Specifies the delay in microseconds (us) between the reception of the trigger signal and the trigger activation.
Float.Description: Specifies the delay in microseconds (us) between the reception of the trigger signal and the trigger activation. Further Trigger pulses arriving during the delay are not processed.

Integer.Name: TriggerDivider
Integer.ToolTip: If TriggerDivider is set to a value x > 1, only every x-th source signal will activate the trigger.
Integer.Description: If TriggerDivider is set to a value x > 1, only every x-th source signal will activate the trigger. Using TriggerDivider can be useful for source signals with high frequency. It reduces the signal to the desired frequency for acquisition. A typical example is an encoder.

Boolean.Name: TriggerDividerAvl
Boolean.ToolTip: Availability of the trigger divider feature
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the trigger divider feature is available.

Integer.Name: IS_TRIGGER_TIMEOUT
Integer.ToolTip: The trigger timeout
Integer.Description: The timeout value in ms for triggered image capture. The default value ist 60,000 ms.

Enumeration.Name: ExposureMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets the exposure mode automatically depending on the trigger mode.
Enumeration.Description: Sets the exposure mode automatically depending on the trigger mode.

EnumEntry.Name: Timed
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Timed exposure. The exposure time is set using the ExposureTime or ExposureAuto features.
EnumEntry.Description: Timed exposure. The exposure time is set using the ExposureTime or ExposureAuto features. The exposure starts with the ExposureStart or LineStart trigger signal.

Float.Name: ExposureTime
Float.ToolTip: Sets the Exposure time when ExposureMode is Timed and ExposureAuto is Off.
Float.Description: Sets the Exposure time when ExposureMode is Timed and ExposureAuto is Off. This controls the duration where the photosensitive cells are exposed to light. The value and range of ExposureTime depend on the current value of DeviceClockFrequency. If you increase AcquisitionFrameRate and its new value is not applicable with the value of ExposureTime, ExposureTime will be reduced automatically. If you decrease AcquisitionFrameRate again, ExposureTime stays low and must be manually increased to the desired value.

Enumeration.Name: ExposureAuto
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets the automatic exposure control when ExposureMode is Timed.
Enumeration.Description: Sets the automatic exposure control when ExposureMode is Timed. ExposureAuto can be combined with GainAuto.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Exposure duration is user controlled using ExposureTime.
EnumEntry.Description: Exposure duration is user controlled using ExposureTime.

EnumEntry.Name: Continuous
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Exposure duration is constantly adapted by the camera to maximize the dynamic range.
EnumEntry.Description: Exposure duration is constantly adapted by the camera to maximize the dynamic range.

Enumeration.Name: GainSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the Gain to be configured.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the Gain to be configured. The gains supported by a camera depend on the sensor and may differ.

EnumEntry.Name: All
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Gain will be applied to all channels.
EnumEntry.Description: Gain will be applied to all channels.

EnumEntry.Name: Red
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Gain will be applied to the red channel.
EnumEntry.Description: Gain will be applied to the red channel.

EnumEntry.Name: Green
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Gain will be applied to the green channel.
EnumEntry.Description: Gain will be applied to the green channel.

EnumEntry.Name: Blue
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Gain will be applied to the blue channel.
EnumEntry.Description: Gain will be applied to the blue channel.

Enumeration.Name: BalanceWhiteAuto
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls the mode for automatic white balancing between the color channels. The white balancing ratios are automatically adjusted.
Enumeration.Description: Controls the mode for automatic white balancing between the color channels. The white balancing ratios are automatically adjusted.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.ToolTip: White balancing is user controlled using BalanceRatioSelector and BalanceRatio.
EnumEntry.Description: White balancing is user controlled using BalanceRatioSelector and BalanceRatio.

EnumEntry.Name: Continuous
EnumEntry.ToolTip: White balancing is constantly adjusted by the device.
EnumEntry.Description: White balancing is constantly adjusted by the device.

Float.Name: Gain
Float.ToolTip: Controls the gain selected in GainSelector.
Float.Description: Controls the gain selected in GainSelector. The gain is an amplification factor applied to all pixel values. Increasing gain, the image will appear brighter. For best image quality, it is recommended to use small gain values and to increase ExposureTime instead. The gain step width depends on the sensor. For manually adjusting the white balance for color cameras, select a color gain with GainSelector and change the Gain value.

Enumeration.Name: GainAuto
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets the automatic gain control.
Enumeration.Description: Sets the automatic gain control. GainAuto can be combined with ExposureAuto. GainAuto modifies AnalogAll gain, if the sensor supports it. Otherwise, DigitalAll gain is used.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.ToolTip: GainAuto is disabled. The user-defined control of the gain is possible with the Gain command.
EnumEntry.Description: GainAuto is disabled. The user-defined control of the gain is possible with the Gain command.

EnumEntry.Name: Continuous
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Gain is constantly adjusted by the camera.
EnumEntry.Description: Gain is constantly adjusted by the camera.

Float.Name: GainDefault
Float.ToolTip: Returns the default value of the gain selected in GainSelector.
Float.Description: Returns the default value of the gain selected in GainSelector.

Command.Name: GainResetToDefault
Command.ToolTip: Resets the gain selected in GainSelector to its default value.
Command.Description: Resets the gain selected in GainSelector to its default value.

Command.Name: ResetGainConfiguration
Command.ToolTip: Resets all gains to their default values.
Command.Description: Resets all gains to their default values.

Enumeration.Name: BlackLevelSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns which BlackLevel is controlled by the black level features.
Enumeration.Description: Returns which BlackLevel is controlled by the black level features.

EnumEntry.Name: All
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Black Level will be applied to all channels.
EnumEntry.Description: Black Level will be applied to all channels.

Float.Name: BlackLevel
Float.ToolTip: Controls the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector.
Float.Description: Controls the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector. The BlackLevel is an offset that applies to all pixel values. BlackLevel always refers to 8 bit depth.

Float.Name: BlackLevelDefault
Float.ToolTip: Returns the default value of the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector.
Float.Description: Returns the default value of the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector.

Command.Name: BlackLevelResetToDefault
Command.ToolTip: Resets the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector to its default value.
Command.Description: Resets the black level selected in BlackLevelSelector to its default value.

Boolean.Name: LogModeAvl
Boolean.Description: Flag indicating whether the Log Mode feature is supported or not.

Enumeration.Name: LogMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls which LogMode is active.
Enumeration.Description: Controls the LogMode to avoid so-called crosstalk effects with very short exposure times of less than 0.1 ms.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Log Mode Off.

EnumEntry.Name: Manual
EnumEntry.Description: Log Mode manual.

EnumEntry.Name: Auto
EnumEntry.Description: Log Mode Auto.

Integer.Name: LogModeManualValue
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the LogModeManualValue
Integer.Description: Controls the LogModeManualValue.

Integer.Name: LogModeManualValueDefault
Integer.ToolTip: Default value for the LogModeManualValue
Integer.Description: Returns the default value for LogModeManualValue.

Command.Name: LogModeManualValueResetToDefault
Command.Description: Resets LogModeManualValue to its default value.
Command.DisplayName: Log Mode Manual Value Reset To Default

Integer.Name: LogModeManualGain
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the LogModeManualGain
Integer.Description: Controls the LogModeManualGain.

Integer.Name: LogModeManualGainDefault
Integer.ToolTip: Default value for LogModeManualGain
Integer.Description: Returns the default value for LogModeManualGain.

Command.Name: LogModeManualGainResetToDefault
Command.Description: Resets LogModeManualGain to its default value.
Command.DisplayName: Log Mode Manual Gain Reset To Default

Integer.Name: BrightnessAutoTargetTolerance
Integer.ToolTip: Tolerance for BrightnessAutoTarget.
Integer.Description: Tolerance for BrightnessAutoTarget (in 8 bit). Defines an acceptance interval that surrounds BrightnessAutoTarget. If the brightness auto algorithm reaches a value within this acceptance interval, the algorithm has converged.

Integer.Name: BrightnessAutoTarget
Integer.ToolTip: Sets the target value for brightness auto features.
Integer.Description: Sets the target value for brightness auto features like ExposureAuto and GainAuto. The percentage of pixels, that must be brighter than BrightnessAutoTarget, is defined in BrightnessAutoPercentile. The value of BrightnessAutoTarget relates to the current PixelFormat.

Enumeration.Name: BrightnessAutoGainLimitMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls if the limits BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax are active.
Enumeration.Description: Controls if the limits BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax are active. When disabled, the range of Gain is only limited by sensor properties. When enabled, the range of Gain is limited additionally by BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax. When a brightness auto features is active, the Gain can vary within this range.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Disables BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax.

EnumEntry.Name: On
EnumEntry.Description: Enables BrightnessAutoGainMin and BrightnessAutoGainMax.

Float.Name: BrightnessAutoGainMax
Float.ToolTip: Maximum limit of Gain when GainAuto is enabled.
Float.Description: Maximum limit of Gain when GainAuto is enabled.

Float.Name: BrightnessAutoGainMin
Float.ToolTip: Minimum limit of Gain when GainAuto is enabled.
Float.Description: Minimum limit of Gain when GainAuto is enabled.

Enumeration.Name: ColorCorrectionMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls if the color correction is active.
Enumeration.Description: Controls if the color correction is active. Color correction is only available, if the selected Pixel Format is a debayered color format. If color correction is active, the Color Correction Matrix is used to enhance color rendering.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Disables color correction.

EnumEntry.Name: Auto
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Enables color correction automatically, if a matching PixelFormat is selected.
EnumEntry.Description: Enables color correction automatically, if a matching PixelFormat is selected.

Enumeration.Name: ColorCorrectionMatrix
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets the matrix for color correction.
Enumeration.Description: Sets the matrix for color correction. Color correction can be enabled or disabled using Color Correction Mode.

EnumEntry.Name: Normal
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix "Normal'.

EnumEntry.Name: BG40
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix 'BG40'.

EnumEntry.Name: HQ
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Predefined color correction matrix for IR cut-off filter for high quality color rendering (HQ filter)
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix for IR cut-off filter for high quality color rendering (HQ filter).

EnumEntry.Name: AGL
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 3000 kelvins
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 3000 kelvins

EnumEntry.Name: TL84
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 4000 kelvins
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 4000 kelvins

EnumEntry.Name: D50
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 5000 kelvins
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 5000 kelvins

EnumEntry.Name: D65
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 6500 kelvins
EnumEntry.Description: Predefined color correction matrix for a white point of 6500 kelvins

Enumeration.Name: ColorCorrectionMatrixValueSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Defines which multiplier (red, green, blue) to use for the given pixel color.
Enumeration.Description: Defines which multiplier (red, green, blue) to use for the given pixel color.

EnumEntry.Name: Gain00
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the red contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the red contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain01
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the green contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the green contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain02
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the blue contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the blue contribution to the red pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain10
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the red contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the red contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain11
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the green contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the green contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain12
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the blue contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the blue contribution to the green pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain20
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the red contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the red contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain21
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the green contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the green contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).

EnumEntry.Name: Gain22
EnumEntry.ToolTip: This is the blue contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).
EnumEntry.Description: This is the blue contribution to the blue pixel (multiplicative factor).

Float.Name: ColorCorrectionMatrixValue
Float.ToolTip: ColorCorrectionMatrixValue
Float.Description: ColorCorrectionMatrixValue

Enumeration.Name: ColorCorrectionStatus
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns the status of the color correction mode (Color Correction is available for colored sensors in debayered pixel format only).
Enumeration.Description: Returns the status of the color correction mode (Color Correction is available for colored sensors in debayered pixel format only).

EnumEntry.Name: Enabled
EnumEntry.Description: Color correction is enabled.

EnumEntry.Name: NotAvailable
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Color correction is not available for the selected PixelFormat.
EnumEntry.Description: Color correction is not available for the selected PixelFormat.

EnumEntry.Name: Disabled
EnumEntry.Description: Color correction is disabled.

Enumeration.Name: HotpixelCorrectionMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Configures the correction of sensor hot pixels.
Enumeration.Description: The correction is performed by the software. The hot pixel list is stored in the camera's non-volatile camera memory.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Disables hot pixel correction.

EnumEntry.Name: Auto
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Enables hot pixel correction using the hot pixel list(s) stored in the non-volatile camera memory.
EnumEntry.Description: Enables hot pixel correction using the hot pixel list(s) stored in the non-volatile camera memory.

Enumeration.Name: LineSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the physical line (or pin) of the I/O connector to configure
Enumeration.Description: Selects the physical line (or pin) of the I/O to configure. The signals can be inverted by activating LineInverter.

EnumEntry.Name: Line0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Physical Input Line that serves as trigger input.
EnumEntry.Description: Physical Input Line that serves as trigger input (LineMode = Input only). The digital input is galvanically isolated using an opto-coupler to protect the camera and the PC against surges. Only DC voltages may be applied to the digital input.

EnumEntry.Name: Line1
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Physical Output Line that serves as flash output.
EnumEntry.Description: Physical Output Line that serves as flash output (LineMode = Output only). The digital output is galvanically isolated using an opto-coupler to protect the camera and the PC against surges. Only DC voltages may be applied to the digital output.

EnumEntry.Name: Line2
EnumEntry.ToolTip: General Purpose I/O (GPIO1) that can be used as input or output.
EnumEntry.Description: General Purpose I/O (GPIO1) that can be used as input or output. To configure the line as input, change LineMode to Input. To configure the line as output, change LineMode to Output. If Line2 is used as output with LineSource FlashActive, the same signal is also applied to Line1. Attention: The General Purpose IO is not potential-free and has no protective circuit. Faulty wiring (overvoltage, undervoltage) could cause damage to the electronics.

EnumEntry.Name: Line3
EnumEntry.ToolTip: General Purpose I/O (GPIO2) that can be used as input or output.
EnumEntry.Description: General Purpose I/O (GPIO2) that can be used as input or output. To configure the line as input, change LineMode to Input. To configure the line as output, change LineMode to Output. If Line3 is used as output with LineSource FlashActive, the same signal is also applied to Line1. Attention: The General Purpose IO is not potential-free and has no protective circuit. Faulty wiring (overvoltage, undervoltage) could cause damage to the electronics.

Enumeration.Name: LineMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls if the physical line is used as input or output.
Enumeration.Description: Controls if the physical line is used as input or output.

EnumEntry.Name: Input
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The selected physical line is used as input.
EnumEntry.Description: The selected physical line is used as input.

EnumEntry.Name: Output
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The selected physical line is used as output.
EnumEntry.Description: The selected physical line is used as output.

Boolean.Name: LineInverter
Boolean.ToolTip: Controls whether the selected line signal is inverted.
Boolean.Description: Controls whether the selected line signal is inverted. LineInverter affects only output lines with line source FlashActive or UserOutput.

Boolean.Name: LineStatus
Boolean.ToolTip: Returns the current status of the selected input or output line.
Boolean.Description: Returns the current status of the selected input or output line.

Integer.Name: LineStatusAll
Integer.ToolTip: Returns the current status of all available line signals.
Integer.Description: Returns the current status of all available line signals at time of polling in a single bitfield. Status changes due to FlashActive are not considered.

Enumeration.Name: LineSource
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which internal acquisition or I/O source signal to output on the selected Line
Enumeration.Description: Selects which internal acquisition or I/O source signal to output on the selected line. LineSource is only applied when LineMode is set to Output.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Line output is disabled.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput0
EnumEntry.Description: The current value of UserOutput0.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput1
EnumEntry.Description: The current value of UserOutput1.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput2
EnumEntry.Description: The current value of UserOutput2.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput3
EnumEntry.Description: The current value of UserOutput3.

EnumEntry.Name: FlashActive
EnumEntry.Description: The "FlashActive" signal is HIGH.

EnumEntry.Name: Line1Signal
EnumEntry.Description: Mirrors the signal of Line 1, e.g. "Flash Active".

EnumEntry.Name: PWM0
EnumEntry.Description: The output of PWM0.

Enumeration.Name: UserOutputSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which bit of the User Output register will be set by UserOutputValue
Enumeration.Description: Selects which bit of the User Output register will be set by UserOutputValue.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects the bit 0 of the User Output register.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the bit 0 of the User Output register.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput1
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects the bit 1 of the User Output register.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the bit 1 of the User Output register.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput2
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects the bit 2 of the User Output register.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the bit 2 of the User Output register.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput3
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects the bit 3 of the User Output register.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the bit 3 of the User Output register.

Boolean.Name: UserOutputValue
Boolean.ToolTip: Sets the value of the bit selected by UserOutputSelector.
Boolean.Description: Sets the value of the bit selected by UserOutputSelector.

Integer.Name: UserOutputValueAll
Integer.ToolTip: Sets the values of all bits of the User Output register
Integer.Description: Sets the values of all bits of the User Output register. It is subject to the UserOutputValueAllMask.

Integer.Name: UserOutputValueAllMask
Integer.ToolTip: Sets the write mask to apply to the value specified by UserOutputValueAll before writing it in the User Output register
Integer.Description: Sets the write mask to apply to the value specified by UserOutputValueAll before writing it in the User Output register. If the UserOutputValueAllMask feature is present, setting the user Output register using UserOutputValueAll will only change the bits that have a corresponding bit in the mask set to one.

Enumeration.Name: PWMSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which pulse width modulation module to configure.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which pulse width modulation module to configure.

EnumEntry.Name: PWM0
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the PWM0 module.

Enumeration.Name: PWMTriggerSource
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the internal signal or physical input Line that will be the source to start the PWM.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the internal signal or physical input Line that will be the source to start the PWM. On default, the PWM is active as long as the signal is HIGH. Changing the PWMTriggerActivation to LevelLow, the PWM is active as long as the signal is LOW.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: Disables the PWM.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The PWM is active while the UserOutput0 is set to True.
EnumEntry.Description: The PWM is active while the UserOutput0 is set to True.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput1
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The PWM is active while the UserOutput1 is set to True.
EnumEntry.Description: The PWM is active while the UserOutput1 is set to True.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput2
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The PWM is active while the UserOutput2 is set to True.
EnumEntry.Description: The PWM is active while the UserOutput2 is set to True.

EnumEntry.Name: UserOutput3
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The PWM is active while the UserOutput3 is set to True.
EnumEntry.Description: The PWM is active while the UserOutput3 is set to True.

Enumeration.Name: PWMTriggerActivation
Enumeration.ToolTip: Specifies the activation mode of the PWMTriggerSource signal.
Enumeration.Description: Specifies the activation mode of the PWMTriggerSource signal.

EnumEntry.Name: LevelHigh
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid as long as the level of the PWMTriggerSource signal is HIGH.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid as long as the level of the PWMTriggerSource signal is HIGH.

EnumEntry.Name: LevelLow
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid as long as the level of the PWMTriggerSource signal is LOW.
EnumEntry.Description: Specifies that the trigger is considered valid as long as the level of the PWMTriggerSource signal is LOW.

Float.Name: PWMFrequency
Float.ToolTip: Specifies the frequency of the PWM pulse signal.
Float.Description: Specifies the frequency of the PWM pulse signal. The unit of the frequency is Hz.

Integer.Name: PWMDutyCycle
Integer.ToolTip: Specifies the duty cycle of the PWM.
Integer.Description: Specifies the duty cycle of the PWM. Defines the fraction of one pulse (in %) in which the PWM signal is HIGH.

Float.Name: FlashStartDelay
Float.ToolTip: Sets the delay (in microseconds) for the start of the "FlashActive" signal.
Float.Description: Sets the delay (in microseconds) for the start of the "FlashActive" signal. The delay is relative to the reference signal set in FlashReference.

Float.Name: FlashDuration
Float.ToolTip: Sets the duration (in microseconds) of the "FlashActive" signal
Float.Description: Sets the duration (in microseconds) of the "FlashActive" signal. If the value is 0 the "FlashActive" signal is off.

Enumeration.Name: FlashReference
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the internal signal that will be the source to start the "FlashActive" signal.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the internal signal that will be the source to start the "FlashActive" signal.

EnumEntry.Name: ExposureActive
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Flash is active during the exposure of images.
EnumEntry.Description: Flash is active during the exposure of images.

EnumEntry.Name: ExposureStart
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Flash refers to the start of exposure and is defined by the flash start delay and flash duration.
EnumEntry.Description: Flash refers to the start of exposure and is defined by the flash start delay and flash duration.

EnumEntry.Name: GlobalFlashWindow
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The "GlobalFlashWindow" flashes when all rows of a rolling shutter sensor are exposed simultaneously.
EnumEntry.Description: The "GlobalFlashWindow" flashes when all rows of a rolling shutter sensor are exposed simultaneously.

Integer.Name: PayloadSize
Integer.ToolTip: Provides the number of bytes transferred for each image or chunk on the stream channel.
Integer.Description: Provides the number of bytes transferred for each image on the stream channel. This is the total size of data payload for a data block.

Enumeration.Name: UserSetSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the user set that has to be loaded, saved or configured.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the user set that has to be loaded, saved or configured. Loading a user set can affect the availability and behavior of multiple camera features.

EnumEntry.Name: Default
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the default setting. Loading the user set Default guarantees a state where a continuous acquisition is started that uses only the mandatory features. It is compatible with most applications.

EnumEntry.Name: UserSet0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: Selects the user defined setting UserSet0.
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the user defined setting UserSet0.

Command.Name: UserSetLoad
Command.ToolTip: Loads the user set that is specified by UserSetSelector to the camera and activates it.
Command.Description: Loads the user set that is specified by UserSetSelector to the camera and activates it. The access changes to not-available during an acquisition. And the access is not-available for user-defined user sets (UserSet0) as long as no camera settings have yet been saved via UserSetSave for those user-defined user sets.

Command.Name: UserSetSave
Command.ToolTip: Saves the camera parameters into the user defined user set that is specified by UserSetSelector.
Command.Description: Saves the camera parameters into the user defined user set that is specified by UserSetSelector. The access changes to not-available during an acquisition. And the access changes to not-available if a predefined user set, e.g. Default, is specified by UserSetSelector.

String.Name: UEyeParametersetPath
String.Description: Filepath to uEye parameterset (.ini) file, which to load/save.

Command.Name: UEyeParametersetLoad
Command.ToolTip: Loads the parameterset from the specified file path
Command.Description: This will load the uEye parameterset file (.ini) from the specified path.

Command.Name: UEyeParametersetSave
Command.ToolTip: Saves the parameterset to the specified file path
Command.Description: This will save the uEye paremeterset file(.ini) to the specified path.

Enumeration.Name: LEDSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the LED to be controlled.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the LED to be controlled.

EnumEntry.Name: LEDStatus
EnumEntry.Description: Selects the status LED.

Enumeration.Name: LEDTriggerSource
Enumeration.ToolTip: Controls the selected LED.
Enumeration.Description: Controls the selected LED.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: The LED is disabled.

EnumEntry.Name: CameraStatus
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The LED signals the camera status.
EnumEntry.Description: The LED signals the camera status.

EnumEntry.Name: BlinkingFast
EnumEntry.Description: The LED blinks fast.

Enumeration.Name: FileSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the target file in the device.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the target file in the device.

EnumEntry.Name: UserData1
EnumEntry.Description: User-defined data file with max. 64 byte size.

EnumEntry.Name: UserData2
EnumEntry.Description: User-defined data file with max. 64 kB size.

Enumeration.Name: FileOperationSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the target operation for the selected file in the camera.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the target operation for the selected file in the camera. This operation is executed when the FileOperationExecute feature is called.

EnumEntry.Name: Open
EnumEntry.Description: Opens the file selected by FileSelector in the camera. The access mode in which the file is opened is selected by FileOpenMode.

EnumEntry.Name: Close
EnumEntry.Description: Closes the file selected by FileSelector in the camera.

EnumEntry.Name: Read
EnumEntry.Description: Reads data from the camera storage into FileAccessBuffer. The data is read from the file with relative offset FileAccessOffset and has the length FileAccessLength.

EnumEntry.Name: Write
EnumEntry.Description: Writes data from FileAccessBuffer into the camera storage. The written data has the length FileAccessLength. For this operation, FileAccessOffset is not available. The complete file must be written all at once.

EnumEntry.Name: Delete
EnumEntry.Description: Deletes the file selected by FileSelector in the camera. This operation does not remove the associated FileSelector entry.

Command.Name: FileOperationExecute
Command.Description: Executes the operation selected by FileOperationSelector on the file selected by FileSelector.

Enumeration.Name: FileOpenMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the access mode in which a file is opened in the camera.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the access mode in which a file is opened in the camera.

EnumEntry.Name: Read
EnumEntry.Description: Read-only open mode.

EnumEntry.Name: Write
EnumEntry.Description: Write-only open mode.

Enumeration.Name: FileOpenStatus
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns the current status of the file selected by FileSelector.
Enumeration.Description: Returns the current status of the file selected by FileSelector.

EnumEntry.Name: Close
EnumEntry.Description: The file is closed.

EnumEntry.Name: OpenRead
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The file is open with read-only access.
EnumEntry.Description: The file is open with read-only access.

EnumEntry.Name: OpenWrite
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The file is open with write-only access.
EnumEntry.Description: The file is open with write-only access.

Integer.Name: FileAccessOffset
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the offset of the FileAccessBuffer.
Integer.Description: Controls the offset of the FileAccessBuffer relative to the beginning of the selected file in the camera storage. This feature is only available when a file was opened and FileOperationSelector is set to Read.

Integer.Name: FileAccessLength
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the length of the mapping between the device file storage and the FileAccessBuffer.
Integer.Description: Controls the length of the mapping between the device file storage and the FileAccessBuffer. The FileAccessLength defines the number of bytes to transfer to or from the FileAccessBuffer. This feature is only available when a file was opened and FileOperationSelector is set to Read or Write.

Enumeration.Name: FileOperationStatus
Enumeration.ToolTip: Represents the file operation execution status.
Enumeration.Description: Represents the file operation execution status.

EnumEntry.Name: Success
EnumEntry.Description: File Operation was successful.

EnumEntry.Name: Failure
EnumEntry.Description: File Operation failed.

Integer.Name: FileOperationResult
Integer.ToolTip: Returns the number of successfully read or written bytes.
Integer.Description: Returns the number of successfully read or written bytes if FileOperationSelector is set to Read or Write.

Integer.Name: FileSize
Integer.ToolTip: Represents the size of the selected file in bytes.
Integer.Description: Represents the size of the selected file in bytes.

Register.Name: FileAccessBuffer
Register.Description: Defines the intermediate access buffer that allows the exchange of data between the camera file storage and the application. This register mapped FileAccessBuffer must be written with the target data before executing a write operation. For read operation, the data can be read from the FileAccessBuffer after the read operation has been executed. The effective data transfer is done upon FileOperationExecute execution. The maximum length of FileAccessBuffer depends on the camera hardware.

Command.Name: I2COperationExecute
Command.ToolTip: Executes the operation selected by I2COperationSelector.
Command.Description: Executes the operation selected by I2COperationSelector.

Enumeration.Name: I2COperationStatus
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns the current status of the I2C communication.
Enumeration.Description: Returns the current status of the I2C communication.

EnumEntry.Name: Ready
EnumEntry.Description: I2C communication is ready.

EnumEntry.Name: Error
EnumEntry.Description: An error occurred while sending.

EnumEntry.Name: TimeoutError
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The acknowledge signal after writing was not received.
EnumEntry.Description: The acknowledge signal after writing was not received within the given time defined in I2CAckPollingTimeout.

EnumEntry.Name: InvalidDeviceAddress
EnumEntry.ToolTip: An invalid I2C Device Address was specified.
EnumEntry.Description: An invalid I2C Device Address was specified.

Enumeration.Name: I2CMode
Enumeration.ToolTip: Returns the I2C mode that determines the speed grade of I2C communication.
Enumeration.Description: Returns the I2C mode that determines the speed grade of I2C communication.

EnumEntry.Name: StandardMode
EnumEntry.ToolTip: I2C standard mode with 100 kHz clock frequency.
EnumEntry.Description: I2C standard mode with 100 kHz clock frequency

EnumEntry.Name: FastMode
EnumEntry.ToolTip: I2C fast mode with 400 kHz clock frequency.
EnumEntry.Description: I2C fast mode with 400 kHz clock frequency.

EnumEntry.Name: FastModePLUS
EnumEntry.ToolTip: I2C Fast-mode plus with 1 MHz clock frequency.
EnumEntry.Description: I2C Fast-mode plus with 1 MHz clock frequency

Enumeration.Name: I2COperationSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects the operation for the I2C communication.
Enumeration.Description: Selects the operation for the I2C communication. This operation is executed when I2COperationExecute is called. The I2C operation is targeted at the register with IC2RegisterAddress of the device with I2CDeviceAddress.

EnumEntry.Name: Read
EnumEntry.Description: I2C read command. Reads the data from the selected register of the device into I2CData.

EnumEntry.Name: Write
EnumEntry.Description: I2C write command. Writes the data from I2CData into the selected register of the device.

Integer.Name: I2CDeviceAddressLength
Integer.ToolTip: Returns the length of I2CDeviceAddress in bits.
Integer.Description: Returns the length of I2CDeviceAddress in bits. Currently, only addresses with 7-bit length are supported.

Integer.Name: I2CRegisterAddressLength
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the length of I2CRegisterAddress in bytes.
Integer.Description: Controls the length of I2CRegisterAddress in bytes.

Enumeration.Name: I2CRegisterAddressEndianness
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects Endianness of the Register Address.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which byte of the Register Address to transfer first on I2C bus.

EnumEntry.Name: LittleEndian
EnumEntry.Description: LittleEndian: Transmit the lowest significant byte first.

EnumEntry.Name: BigEndian
EnumEntry.Description: BigEndian: Transmit the highest significant byte first.

Integer.Name: I2CDataLength
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the length of I2CData in bytes.
Integer.Description: Controls the length of I2CData in bytes.

Integer.Name: I2CDeviceAddress
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the address of the I2C device.
Integer.Description: Controls the address of the I2C device. The maximum value of I2CDeviceAddress is limited by I2CDeviceAddressLength. Currently, only addresses with 7-bit length are supported.

Integer.Name: I2CRegisterAddress
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the address of the destination register for the operation selected by I2COperationSelector.
Integer.Description: Controls the address of the destination register for the operation selected by I2COperationSelector.

Boolean.Name: I2CAckPollingEnable
Boolean.Description: Enables the I2C acknowledge polling. When I2CAckPollingEnable is true, the camera waits for an acknowledgement after an I2C write operation.

Integer.Name: I2CData
Integer.ToolTip: Controls the I2C data that is subject to the selected I2C operation.
Integer.Description: Controls the I2C data that is subject to the selected I2C operation. If I2COperationSelector is set to Read, I2CData returns the data read from the I2C device after I2COperationExecute was executed. If I2COperationSelector is set to Write, I2CData controls the data that will be sent to the I2C device when I2COperationExecute is executed. The maximum value of I2CData is limited by I2CDataLength. The access changes to read-only when I2COperationSelector is set to Read.

Register.Name: I2CTransmitBuffer
Register.ToolTip: Data to be sent to I2C or read from I2C
Register.Description: Data to be sent to I2C or read from I2C

Enumeration.Name: OpticControllerSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Selects which optic controller to configure.
Enumeration.Description: Selects which optic controller to configure.

EnumEntry.Name: OpticController0
EnumEntry.Description: Selects Optic Controller 0

Enumeration.Name: FocusAuto
Enumeration.ToolTip: Sets automatic focus.
Enumeration.Description: Sets automatic focus. If enabled, the optic device shall be set into a mode of operation, where the lens automatically finds the best possible focus.

EnumEntry.Name: Off
EnumEntry.Description: The focus is set manually.

EnumEntry.Name: Continuous
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The focus is constantly adapted by the device to maximize the dynamic range.
EnumEntry.Description: The focus is constantly adapted by the device to maximize the dynamic range.

Integer.Name: FocusStepper
Integer.ToolTip: FocusStepper controls the stepper value of the focus.
Integer.Description: FocusStepper controls the stepper value of the focus. E.g. ObjectSensorDistance or FocalPower. 0 is the closest focus.

Boolean.Name: SubRegionFollowSource
Boolean.ToolTip: Defines whether the subregion corresponds to the ROI or is only a part of the ROI.
Boolean.Description: Defines whether the subregion corresponds to the ROI or is only a part of the ROI. Values description True: The sub region covers the region selected in SubRegionSourceSelector. False: The sub region can be limited to a ROI relative to the region selected in SubRegionSourceSelector.

Integer.Name: SubRegionHeight
Integer.ToolTip: Height of the selected sub region.
Integer.Description: Height of the selected sub region. Only available when SubRegionFollowSource = False

Integer.Name: SubRegionOffsetX
Integer.ToolTip: Horizontal offset in pixels from the origin of the ROI to the selected sub region.
Integer.Description: Horizontal offset in pixels from the origin of the ROI to the selected sub region. Only available when SubRegionFollowSource = False

Integer.Name: SubRegionOffsetY
Integer.ToolTip: Vertical offset in pixels from the origin of the ROI to the selected sub region.
Integer.Description: Vertical offset in pixels from the origin of the ROI to the selected sub region. Only available when SubRegionFollowSource = False

Enumeration.Name: SubRegionSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Defines the auto function to be calculated based on the sub region, e.g. ExposureAuto and GainAuto.
Enumeration.Description: Defines the auto function to be calculated based on the sub region, e.g. ExposureAuto and GainAuto. AutoFeatureBrightnessAuto: The image information from the sub region is used for ExposureAuto and GainAuto. AutoFeatureBalanceWhiteAuto: The image information from the sub region is used for AutoWhiteBalance.

EnumEntry.Name: AutoFeatureBrightnessAuto
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The image information from the sub region is used for ExposureAuto and GainAuto.
EnumEntry.Description: The image information from the sub region is used for ExposureAuto and GainAuto.

EnumEntry.Name: AutoFeatureFocusAuto
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The image information from the sub-region is used for FocusAuto.
EnumEntry.Description: The image information from the sub-region is used for FocusAuto.

Enumeration.Name: SubRegionSourceSelector
Enumeration.ToolTip: Defines inside which ROI the sub region is located.
Enumeration.Description: Defines inside which ROI the sub region is located. Values description Region0: The sub region is located in ROI 0.

EnumEntry.Name: Region0
EnumEntry.ToolTip: The sub region is located in ROI 0.
EnumEntry.Description: The sub region is located in ROI 0.

Integer.Name: SubRegionWidth
Integer.ToolTip: Width of the selected sub region.
Integer.Description: Width of the selected sub region. Only available when SubRegionFollowSource = False

Port.Name: Device
Port.ToolTip: Port giving access to the uEye device

Integer.Name: TLParamsLocked
Integer.Description: Used by the Transport Layer to prevent critical features changes during acquisition.
